title: At the Supermarket
description: Shopping for food and paying at the till

rows.label: Supermarket
rows.english: Supermarket — Billa, Lidl, Kaufland, Tesco and Albert are the most common chains in Slovakia

rows.label: Cashier
rows.english: Cashier (female) — most common. Male form: pokladník

rows.label: Plastic bag
rows.english: Plastic bag (you pay a few cents per bag — EU rules. Many bring a reusable taška)

rows.label: Scale
rows.english: Scale — weigh loose fruit and veg yourself, then print the barcode sticker

rows.label: Loyalty card
rows.english: Loyalty card — Billa, Kaufland and Tesco all have free loyalty cards with points and discounts

rows.label: Potato
rows.english: Potatoes (plural — you almost always buy them in bulk)

rows.label: Pepper
rows.english: Pepper / Paprika (also refers to the red spice)

rows.label: Cabbage
rows.english: Cabbage (a staple of Slovak cuisine — kapustnica soup!)

rows.label: Plum
rows.english: Plum — used to make the famous Slovak spirit slivovica (plum brandy)!

rows.label: Grapes
rows.english: Grapes (Slovakia has a long winemaking tradition)

rows.label: Bread
rows.english: Bread (dark rye bread is very popular in Slovakia)

rows.label: Eggs
rows.english: Eggs (plural — sold per 6 or 10, vajce = one egg)

rows.label: Cheese
rows.english: Cheese — look out for oštiepok (smoked sheep cheese), a Slovak speciality!

rows.label: Oil
rows.english: Oil (sunflower oil — slnečnicový olej — is most common)

rows.label: Loyalty card
rows.english: Do you have a loyalty card?

rows.label: Ask for bag
rows.english: I'd like a plastic bag please.

words.english: I'd like a plastic bag please.
Do you have a loyalty card?

scenarios.situation: At the checkout the cashier asks 'Máte vernostnú kartu?' What is she asking?
choices: Do you have a loyalty card?
scenarios.answer: Do you have a loyalty card?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Máte vernostnú kartu? = Do you have a loyalty card? Vernostná karta = loyalty card. Billa, Kaufland and Tesco all offer free loyalty cards with points and weekly member discounts — worth picking up!

scenarios.situation: You picked loose tomatoes and need to weigh them before going to the till. Where do you go?
choices: Straight to the pokladňa with them
To the váha (scale) in the produce section
scenarios.answer: To the váha (scale) in the produce section
scenarios.answerMeaning: Váha = scale. In Slovak supermarkets you weigh loose fruit and zelenina (vegetables) yourself at the self-service scale, print a barcode sticker and stick it on the bag before going to the pokladňa (checkout).

scenarios.situation: You want to pay by card. Which phrase do you say to the cashier?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Platím kartou = I'm paying by card. Platím hotovosťou = I'm paying by cash. Card payment — including contactless — is accepted in virtually all Slovak supermarkets.

scenarios.situation: The cashier says 'Spolu je to osemnásť eur päťdesiat centov.' How much is the total?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Osemnásť eur päťdesiat centov = €18.50. Osemnásť = 18 (from Stage 1, Lesson 5 — Numbers!). Spolu = total / altogether. Päťdesiat = fifty.

scenarios.situation: You need a bag for your shopping. What do you say to the cashier?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Chcem igelitku, prosím = I'd like a plastic bag please. Under EU single-use plastics rules, plastic bags cost a few cents in Slovakia. Many locals bring a reusable taška (bag) to avoid the charge.

scenarios.situation: You see a label on strawberries that says 'Zľava 30%'. What does this mean?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Zľava = discount / sale. Zľava 30% = 30% off. Slovak supermarkets rotate weekly discounts — especially on seasonal ovocie (fruit) and zelenina (vegetables). Loyalty card holders often get extra zľavy.

scenarios.situation: At the self-checkout the screen shows 'Vložte kartu.' What should you do?
choices: Insert your card into the reader
scenarios.answer: Insert your card into the reader
scenarios.answerMeaning: Vložte kartu = Insert (your) card. Vložiť = to insert / to put in. After inserting, the machine will show 'Zadajte PIN' = Enter your PIN. Contactless also works on most terminals — just tap.

scenarios.situation: Which of these is Slovak for 'plum' — the fruit behind Slovakia's famous slivovica brandy?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Slivka = plum. Slivovica (plum brandy) gets its name directly from slivka! Čerešňa = cherry, Hruška = pear, Broskyňa = peach. Slovak orchards are famous for stone fruit — look out for seasonal prices in summer.

scenarios.situation: You only have a few items to buy. Which should you grab — a Nákupný vozík or a Košík?
choices: Nákupný vozík — always use the big trolley
Košík — it's a small basket, perfect for a few items
scenarios.answer: Košík — it's a small basket, perfect for a few items
scenarios.answerMeaning: Košík = shopping basket (for a small shop). Nákupný vozík = shopping trolley / cart (for a big weekly shop). In Slovak supermarkets, baskets are stacked near the entrance — grab one if you're buying just a few things.

scenarios.situation: You want to know how much an item costs before buying it. Which word on the shelf label tells you the price?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Cena = price. Shelf labels in Slovak supermarkets always show the cena per unit (e.g. €1.29/ks — ks = kus = piece) and often the cena per kilogram. Zľava = discount, Váha = weight/scale, Účtenka = receipt.

scenarios.situation: You left your bank card at home. What do you say to the cashier?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Platím hotovosťou = I'm paying by cash. Hotovosť = cash / cash money. Platím kartou = I'm paying by card. Always good to know both — some smaller markets or market stalls may be cash-only (len hotovosť).

scenarios.situation: Your recipe calls for kuracie mäso, hrozno, citrón, and maslo. What are you buying?
scenarios.answerMeaning: Kuracie mäso = chicken meat, Hrozno = grapes, Citrón = lemon, Maslo = butter. A classic combo for a Slovak lemon-butter chicken dish! Maslo (butter) is also essential for Slovak pastries and cakes.

items.sentence: ___ stojí toto mlieko? — Stojí jedno euro deväťdesiat.
items.translation: ___ does this milk cost? — It costs one euro ninety.
I'm paying by ___. I don't have cash on me.

items.sentence: Kde je ___? Chcem si odvážiť paradajky.
items.translation: Where is the ___? I want to weigh the tomatoes.
I'd like a ___ please — I have a lot of shopping.

items.sentence: Na ___ sú jahody lacnejšie pre členov.
items.translation: With a ___ strawberries are cheaper for members.

items.sentence: Potrebujem kúpiť ___ na tortu — recept vyžaduje 200 gramov.
items.translation: I need to buy ___ for the cake — the recipe calls for 200 grams.

items.sentence: ___ mi povedala, že jahody sú dnes v akcii.
items.translation: The ___ told me that strawberries are on sale today.

items.sentence: ___ stojí toto mlieko? — Stojí jedno euro deväťdesiat.
Kde je ___? Chcem si odvážiť paradajky.
Na ___ sú jahody lacnejšie pre členov.
Potrebujem kúpiť ___ na tortu — recept vyžaduje 200 gramov.
___ mi povedala, že jahody sú dnes v akcii.

exercises.scenario: 🛒 You are at the Billa checkout. The cashier helps you pay and pack.

turns.snailMessage: Dobrý deň! Máte vernostnú kartu Billa?
turns.snailMessageEnglish: Good day! Do you have a Billa loyalty card?
turns.snailReply: Ďakujem! Body sú pripísané. Spolu to je dvanásť eur päťdesiat centov.
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Thank you! Points have been added. The total is twelve euros fifty cents.

turns.snailReply: Dobre. Prosím vložte kartu do terminálu.
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Good. Please insert your card into the terminal.

choices.english: Alright. Should I enter my PIN?
I don't have my card with me.
I want to pay with apples.

turns.snailReply: Áno, zadajte PIN priamo na termináli, prosím.
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Yes, please enter your PIN directly on the terminal.

choices.english: Done. Can I get a receipt?
How much does an apple cost?

turns.snailReply: Samozrejme! Tu je vaša účtenka. Chcete igelitku?
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Of course! Here is your receipt. Would you like a bag?

choices.english: Yes, I'd like one bag please.

turns.snailReply: Igelitka stojí päť centov. Pridám to k platbe?
turns.snailReplyEnglish: The bag costs five cents. Shall I add it to the payment?

choices.english: Yes please. What's my total now?

choices.slovak: Nie, igelitka by mala byť zadarmo.
choices.english: No, the bag should be free.

turns.snailReply: Spolu dvanásť eur päťdesiatpäť centov. Platba prebehla úspešne!
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Total twelve euros fifty-five cents. Payment was successful!

turns.snailReply: Príjemný nákup a dobrú chuť! Dovidenia!
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Happy shopping and enjoy your meal! Goodbye!

turns.snailReply: Dovidenia! Tešíme sa na vašu ďalšiu návštevu!
turns.snailReplyEnglish: Goodbye! We look forward to your next visit!

exercises.scenario: 🛒 Tentoraz len po slovensky! Zaplaťte na pokladni v Bille.

turns.snailMessage: Dobrý deň! Máte vernostnú kartu Billa?
turns.snailReply: Ďakujem! Body sú pripísané. Spolu to je dvanásť eur päťdesiat centov.
Dobre. Prosím vložte kartu do terminálu.
Áno, zadajte PIN priamo na termináli, prosím.
Samozrejme! Tu je vaša účtenka. Chcete igelitku?
Igelitka stojí päť centov. Pridám to k platbe?

choices.slovak: Nie, igelitka by mala byť zadarmo.

turns.snailReply: Spolu dvanásť eur päťdesiatpäť centov. Platba prebehla úspešne!
Príjemný nákup a dobrú chuť! Dovidenia!
Dovidenia! Tešíme sa na vašu ďalšiu návštevu!
